2603.27727.title: Suppression of $^{14}\mathrm{C}$ photon hits in large liquid scintillator detectors via spatiotemporal deep learning
2603.27727.paper_summary: Liquid scintillator detectors are widely used in neutrino experiments due to their low energy threshold and high energy resolution. Despite the tiny abundance of $^{14}$C in LS, the photons induced by the $β$ decay of the $^{14}$C isotope inevitably contaminate the signal, degrading the energy resolution. In this work, we propose three models to tag $^{14}$C photon hits in $e^+$ events with $^{14}$C pile-up, thereby suppressing its impact on the energy resolution at the hit level: a gated spatiotemporal graph neural network and two Transformer-based models with scalar and vector charge encoding. For a simulation dataset in which each event contains one $^{14}$C and one $e^+$ with kinetic energy below 5 MeV, the models achieve $^{14}$C recall rates of 25%-48% while maintaining $e^+$ to $^{14}$C misidentification below 1%, leading to a large improvement in the resolution of total charge for events where $e^+$ and $^{14}$C photon hits strongly overlap in space and time.
2603.27727.paper_summary_zh: 液體閃爍體探測器因其低能量閾值和高能量解析度而廣泛應用於中微子實驗。儘管$^{14}$C在液體閃爍體中的豐度微乎其微，但$^{14}$C同位素的$β$衰變所誘發的光子不可避免地污染了信號，降低了能量解析度。在這項工作中，我們提出了三種模型來標記$^{14}$C在$e^+$事件中的光子擊中，並在$^{14}$C堆疊的情況下，從而抑制其對擊中層次能量解析度的影響：一個門控時空圖神經網絡和兩個基於Transformer的模型，分別使用標量和向量電荷編碼。對於一個模擬數據集，其中每個事件包含一個$^{14}$C和一個動能低於5 MeV的$e^+$，這些模型實現了$^{14}$C的召回率為25%-48%，同時保持$e^+$與$^{14}$C的誤識別率低於1%，從而在$e^+$和$^{14}$C光子擊中在空間和時間上強烈重疊的事件中，顯著改善了總電荷的解析度。
2603.27727.author: Junle Li et.al.
2603.27727.authors: Junle Li, Zhaoxiang Wu, Guanda Gong, Zhaohan Li, Wuming Luo, Jiahui Wei, Wenxing Fang, Hehe Fan
